sections.biography-source-undefined:
Sima Yue, styled Yuanchao, was from Wen county, Henei commandery. He was a distant relative of the Sima clan.

He controlled most military affairs during the reign of Sima Chi. He left to go on a northern expedition, but Sima Chi took that opportunity to issue edicts denouncing and urging a campaign against Sima Yue. Anxious, Sima Yue fell ill and died on April 23, 311.

full_text:
Sima Yue (Yuanchao) 司馬越 (元超)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Sima Yue (司馬越), styledYuanchao (元超). The eighth of the Princes associated with the Turmoil of the Eight Princes. Sima Yue was affiliated with the Jin Dynasty.

BiographySource Undefined
Sima Yue, styled Yuanchao, was from Wen county, Henei commandery. He was a distant relative of the Sima clan.
He controlled most military affairs during the reign of Sima Chi. He left to go on a northern expedition, but Sima Chi took that opportunity to issue edicts denouncing and urging a campaign against Sima Yue. Anxious, Sima Yue fell ill and died on April 23, 311.
